mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) in periods where RSAFS growth is positive and accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.3pp in 3 months during an economic contraction.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains relatively low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which typically supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) when CPIAUCSL growth is high and FEDFUNDS is low, particularly during inflationary growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, a positive surprise in housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust investment and consumer confidence, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 reactions over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following HOUST positive surprises during strong economic expansion in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which underpins corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer, quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) when INDPRO growth is positive and accelerating over a 6-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth, particularly below a critical threshold, signals a weakening job market, which dampens consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100,000 during a slowing growth regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is actively rising while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is stable or declining, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at cooling the economy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or flat (average return > 0.01%) when FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL growth is low during a monetary tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction or recession, a positive surprise in GDP (GDPC1) growth, even if still negative, can signal a bottoming process and lead to a relief rally in the S&P 500 as expectations improve over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth turns positive after at least two consecutive negative quarters
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain negative or flat following the first positive GDPC1 print after a recession in more than 50% of observed recovery periods.
